compiled_state.author: Claude

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: DESIGN DIVERGENCE — NOT DONE, DO NOT LAND. The decomposition I implemented diverged from the approved epic ACs on 4 points (authorship=raw bytes not in-toto Statement; key validity=HLC-epoch not git-commit-ancestry [SECURITY]; ledger schema; missing key_not_valid_at_era verdict). Epic completion-verifier correctly FAILED on AC8/AC9. Transition stories filed as children: TS1 c96d (in-toto authorship), TS2 09f9 (git-commit-ancestry era — hardest, security-critical), TS3 117b (ledger+verdict), TS4 b134 (docs). Ordered TS1->TS2->TS3->TS4. Do NOT submit the Gerrit stack (558/560/561/563/576/579/580/581) until the transition lands. Full plan + state in session_log d7c0 (insidious-demonian-harborseal).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
TRANSITION REMEDIATION COMPLETE (TS1-4 done, closed, all 8 Gerrit changes LLM-Review+1 AND Verified+1), but EPIC completion-verifier FAILS on 3 ACs BEYOND the 4-delta remediation scope — NOT covered by any transition story:
- AC1: no write-time guard rejecting PRIVATE-KEY material in an identity event (missing since original ddbe/change 558). Small security hardening.
- AC7: enforcement CLI diverges from spec — delivered 'rebar verify-authorship' (config-driven, TEXT output) vs AC7's 'rebar verify-identity --require-authenticated --since <identity.enforce_since>' with JSON report entries {event_uuid,ticket_id,commit,author,verdict,display}, cutover grandfathering, and a CI workflow file. Spirit met (opt-in gate, exits non-zero, replay-never-rejects, fsck/show grouping) but letter unbuilt. Substantial.
- AC10: key_not_valid_at_era must be added to verify_signature_result.schema.json per the AC; TS3 instead kept it as a separate verify-authorship verdict vocabulary (the TS3 code-review flagged the shared schema as the 'wrong command'). Design tension.
These 3 were pre-existing gaps in the DELIVERED stack, outside the 4 flagged deltas (authorship-payload/era-model/ledger/verdict). NOT submitting the stack pending a scope decision: (A) land the 4 green fixes now + file AC1/AC7/AC10 as follow-on stories; (B) build AC1/AC7/AC10 before landing; (C) reconcile/waive over-specified AC7 sub-parts + do the small AC1+AC10. Awaiting direction.

compiled_state.description:
# Authenticated identity — entity + authorship certification + provider-neutral user mapping

## Context
rebar has no real concept of **who** performed an operation: `author` is `git config user.name`, an
unauthenticated free string anyone with push access to `origin/tickets` can forge. Two needs follow:
(1) attribute ticket events to a **real person** so activity is auditable and non-repudiable, and
(2) map that person to **external ticket-system users** (Jira now; GitHub Issues / Linear later) so
sync assigns work to the right person. **Beneficiary:** projects syncing to external trackers (correct
assignee/reporter attribution, wrong or lost today) and anyone auditing "who did this." This epic
depends on the **asymmetric attestation foundation** for its signing substrate.

## Approach (and why)
Adopt **git-bug's** identity model on rebar's existing event machinery, filling its gaps:
- **Identity = a first-class entity** whose event log IS its version chain (rebar already has
  event-sourcing / replay / snapshot). Stable **opaque rebar id** as the handle — **not**
  self-certifying; the signing key provides tamper-evidence, not the id. Attributes: `name`, `email`,
  `{provider, external_id}` external mappings, and **public** keys only.
- **Attribution always** (name+email denormalized on every event, git-style) + an optional
  `author_id` reference + an optional **authorship signature** — a new attestation kind on the
  foundation's substrate (namespace `rebar.authorship.v1`).
- **Trust root = the identity's in-band public keys**, TOFU on the genesis key + **G2** (key add/
  rotate/revoke must be signed by a currently-valid key). Safe for authorship: low authority, cannot
  forge another person's signatures; the worst case from a bad TOFU binding is a display-name dispute.
- **Provider-neutral mapping.** `{provider, external_id}` keys on the provider's **opaque stable id**,
  never email. Jira is the **first adapter** behind a provider seam; an unmapped inbound user mints a
  **placeholder** that idempotently auto-upgrades.
- **Deliberate creation.** A normal write never auto-mints an identity **entity**; identities are
  created explicitly or as a mapping target (see the default-install invariant below).

## Mechanisms (concrete definitions — these resolve the load-bearing terms)
- **Private key material.** Authorship signing uses the developer's **existing SSH private key** via
  `ssh-agent` / `~/.ssh`; it is **never** stored in the repo or on the `tickets` branch. The identity
  entity stores only **public** keys, and a write-time guard rejects any attempt to place private-key
  material into an identity event.
- **Era (key-validity window) = position in the `tickets`-branch commit graph, refined by intra-commit
  index.** rebar's HLC is **per-ticket** (`hlc.next_tick(tracker, ticket_id)` in
  `src/rebar/_commands/_seam.py`), so it gives no global cross-ticket sequence. Events reach the
  `tickets` branch either singly (`event_append.stage_and_commit`, one event per commit) or in bulk
  (`event_append.batch_stage_and_commit`, **N events in one commit**), so commit **ancestry** is the
  global merged order, **refined by a stable `intra_idx`** (an event's 0-based rank
  within its commit when that commit's event files are sorted by `(timestamp, uuid)` — a deterministic,
  insertion-independent order computed from the committed events themselves, not git's lexicographic
  tree order; 0 for single-event commits; nothing extra is stored). Each event therefore has a position `(commit, intra_idx)`, and
  the same commit-graph is what git-bug uses for `ValidKeysAtTime`. Each key record binds
  `{pubkey, added_at:(commit,intra_idx), revoked_at:(commit,intra_idx)?}`. Key K is **valid for** an
  event E iff E's position is **>= added_at and < revoked_at** (when set), where positions compare by
  commit ancestry first (`git merge-base --is-ancestor`) and, when two positions share a commit, by
  `intra_idx` — no wall-clock, no fabricated global sequence. Multi-clone reconcile yields one merged
  graph, so validity is deterministic once merged; the authoritative check runs on that merged branch.
- **Verification state — reuse the existing `verdict` enum (do not invent a parallel one).** The
  authorship result reuses the 4-value verdict enum already defined in
  `src/rebar/schemas/verify_signature_result.schema.json` — `certified`, `unsigned`, `foreign_key`,
  `mismatch` — **extended** with one new value `key_not_valid_at_era` (added to that same schema). The
  human-facing `show`/`fsck` display collapses these into a trichotomy: **verified** = `certified`;
  **unsigned** = `unsigned`; **unverified** = `foreign_key` | `mismatch` | `key_not_valid_at_era`.
  There is **no** separate reason field — the `verdict` value IS the reason.
- **The "merge gate" (the real control).** rebar's `tickets` branch auto-pushes to `origin/tickets`
  with no GitHub pre-receive hook, so enforcement is a **CI verification job** — a GitHub Actions
  workflow on the `tickets` branch that runs `rebar verify-identity` / `fsck` over the **merged** log
  and **fails the check** (non-zero exit + a structured per-event report → red commit status / alert)
  on any `unsigned`/`unverified` event **when** the project sets `identity.require_authenticated=true`.
  This is **detect-and-fail-on-the-merged-log**, not pre-receive rejection (the honest limitation of a
  hookless remote); the local write-gate is only a fast-fail UX affordance. Enforcement is scoped by a
  **cutover ref** `identity.enforce_since` (a `tickets`-branch commit recorded when the project first
  enables enforcement): the CI job runs `verify-identity --require-authenticated --since <enforce_since>`,
  so only events at/after the cutover can fail — pre-cutover events are **grandfathered** (reported,
  not failed). Deployment prerequisite: enable the workflow, set `identity.require_authenticated=true`,
  and record `identity.enforce_since` at the enabling commit.
- **Authorship attestation shape.** The DSSE envelope is `{payload, payloadType, signatures}` — DSSE
  has **no `subject` field** (that is an in-toto concept). The **payload** is an in-toto-style Statement
  whose `subject` binds the signed event as `{event_uuid, content_hash}`, with
  `payloadType = "application/vnd.rebar.authorship.v1+json"`; the signature covers
  `PAE(payloadType, payload)`. Because the binding lives in the Statement `subject`, a signature cannot
  be lifted to another event. If the signing key is unavailable at write time, the event is authored
  **unsigned** (Tier 0) — the write never blocks.
- **Placeholder create-path.** The identity create path supports **system-minted placeholders** (no
  user present, no keys, partial data): `create_placeholder(provider, external_id, display_name)`,
  owned by the entity child and consumed by the inbound-ghost child.
- **Modify-Reporter constraint.** Setting the Jira `reporter` field via REST requires the project's
  Jira role to hold the **"Modify Reporter"** permission; without it, reporter sync degrades to
  assignee-only (documented), never hard-fails.
- **Default-install invariant.** With zero identity configuration, behavior is byte-identical to
  pre-feature: a write succeeds with the denormalized git author (name+email — this is the git commit
  author, already public in commit metadata, so it is **not** new email exposure; the "no broadcast"
  property is specifically that **no identity entity with keys/mappings is auto-minted** by a normal
  write), and `list`/`ready`/gates behave identically.

- **`verify-identity` CLI (the enforcement tool).** `rebar verify-identity [--require-authenticated]
  [--since <ref>] [--format text|json]` scans events on the merged `tickets` log (optionally since a
  ref), computes each event's verification state, and emits one **report entry** per event (all events
  in `--format json`; only non-`verified` in text). **Exit code:** non-zero iff any `unsigned`/
  `unverified` event is found under `--require-authenticated`; `0` otherwise (advisory without the
  flag). The CI merge-gate job runs it with `--require-authenticated`.
- **Report-entry schema.** `{event_uuid, ticket_id, commit (tickets-branch SHA), author (name+email),
  author_id?, verdict: <a verify_signature_result verdict, incl. the new key_not_valid_at_era>,
  display: "verified"|"unverified"|"unsigned"}`. `fsck` embeds the same entries; the `verdict` is the
  single source of the failure reason (no separate reason field).
- **Compaction survival (concrete).** A SNAPSHOT records, per folded signed event, a verification tuple
  `{event_uuid, content_hash, signature, signer_pubkey, position:(commit,intra_idx)}`, so a compacted signed event
  re-verifies (signature checked against the recorded `content_hash`; era resolved by comparing the
  recorded event `position` against the signer key's `added_at`/`revoked_at`) without the original event
  object. `process_snapshot` surfaces these tuples; a
  round-trip test asserts a compacted signed event still returns `verified`.
- **TOFU genesis base case.** The genesis key-add is **self-signed** — signed by the very key it
  introduces — and its `added_at_commit` is the identity's genesis commit; it is valid for every event
  whose commit is a descendant of (or equal to) that genesis commit. This is the base case of the G2
  "signed by a currently-valid key" rule (the recursion bottoms out at the self-signed genesis = TOFU);
  every later key change must be signed by an already-valid key.
- **Jira bootstrap availability + fallback.** Seeding `accountId` from email via
  `GET /rest/api/3/user/search` requires the integration's Jira role to hold **"Browse users and
  groups"** and returns nothing when the target's email is privacy-hidden — so it is best-effort with
  an explicit fallback chain: (1) an operator-provided mapping (a config/CSV of `identity → accountId`);
  (2) resolve `accountId` from existing Jira data where the user is already assignee/reporter on synced
  issues; (3) else degrade to project-default assignee (`-1`)/unassigned and leave the mapping unset for
  later repair. Never hard-fail, never block a sync.

## Scope
- **In:** the `identity` entity (a new ticket-type on the `session_log` exemption pattern); event
  attribution fields; the authorship attestation kind on the foundation's substrate; the TOFU + G2 key
  chain with era-scoped validity; the provider-neutral mapping + Jira adapter + placeholder/auto-upgrade;
  reconciler outbound assignee/reporter + inbound ghost; opt-in enforcement (local write-gate + the CI
  merge-gate verify job + fsck marking); snapshot/compaction carrying folded identities + signatures;
  docs + ADR.
- **Out:** the signing substrate itself (foundation epic); operation-cert behavior (op-cert epic);
  DSSE transparency-log / keyless follow-ons; **actual** GitHub Issues / Linear sync (the adapter seam
  must not preclude them, but wiring them is separate).

## Success Criteria
- A person running rebar has a modeled identity; events attribute it (name+email always; an authorship
  signature when opted in), verified offline against the identity's keys.
- An opt-in project can REQUIRE authenticated authorship: `unsigned`/`unverified` events are flagged by
  fsck and fail the CI merge-gate check, while replay never rejects (availability preserved).
- The identity provides a stable local↔`accountId` map the reconciler uses to set the Jira assignee
  (and reporter where the Modify-Reporter permission is held), degrading gracefully when `accountId`
  can't be resolved; unmapped inbound Jira users become auto-upgrading placeholders.
- Key rotation works: events verify against the keys whose validity-ancestry contains them (added_at_commit ancestor, revoked_at_commit not); a revoked key
  cannot sign new events.
- Default install is byte-identical to pre-feature; signing and enforcement are fully opt-in.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A new `identity` ticket-type exists and is exempt from lifecycle / gates / graph / Jira-sync
      (mirrors `session_log`); a create path mints one carrying name, email, `{provider,external_id}`
      mappings, and **public** keys, and a `create_placeholder(provider, external_id, display_name)`
      path mints a keyless partial-data placeholder. Verified by creating both, asserting `show`
      renders the fields, and asserting exclusion from `ready`/`next_batch`/default `list`.
- [ ] Every mutating event carries a denormalized `author` name + email; an optional `author_id`
      references an identity. Regression test: existing events without `author_id`/email still replay
      to the same state.
- [ ] An `authorship` kind wraps an in-toto Statement payload (its `subject` binds `{event_uuid,
      content_hash}`) in a DSSE envelope (`payloadType application/vnd.rebar.authorship.v1+json`), signed
      via the foundation substrate over `PAE(payloadType, payload)`, and
      verifies offline against the identity's in-band keys; a test asserts an event signed by identity A
      does not verify against B, a tampered event fails, and an unavailable signing key yields an
      `unsigned` (Tier-0) write, never a blocked write.
- [ ] Each identity key record binds `{pubkey, added_at_commit, revoked_at_commit?}` (`tickets`-branch
      commit SHAs); key add/rotate/revoke is refused unless signed by a currently-valid key (G2). Using
      two fixture commits on the `tickets` branch, a test asserts the genesis key-add is accepted only when self-signed (TOFU base
      case), an unsigned non-genesis key-add is refused, an event whose commit is NOT a descendant of the key's `added_at_commit` (or IS a descendant of its
      `revoked_at_commit`) fails verification, and one that is a descendant of `added_at_commit` and not
      of `revoked_at_commit` passes.
- [ ] `{provider, external_id}` mappings key on the provider's opaque id; the reconciler resolves an
      outbound Jira assignee by `accountId`, bootstraps via `/user/search` best-effort (which needs the
      "Browse users and groups" permission and may return nothing), then falls back to an
      operator-provided mapping, then to existing Jira assignee/reporter data, and finally degrades to
      project-default (`-1`)/unassigned without hard-failing; reporter is set by `accountId` only when
      the Modify-Reporter permission is held, else assignee-only. Test with a
      fixture identity + stubbed `accountId`.
- [ ] An inbound Jira user with no mapping creates a placeholder via `create_placeholder`; re-running
      resolution after a real mapping is added idempotently upgrades it — a test asserts one identity
      results, not two.
- [ ] Enforcement is opt-in (`identity.require_authenticated`): with it on, a CI merge-gate job runs
      `rebar verify-identity --require-authenticated --since <identity.enforce_since>` over the merged
      log and **exits non-zero**, emitting one report entry (`{event_uuid, ticket_id, commit, author,
      verdict, display}`) per non-`certified` event at/after the cutover, while pre-cutover events are
      grandfathered (test asserts exit code, a report entry's fields, and grandfathering); the local write-gate fails fast but is documented UX-only. Replay never rejects
      (test: a bad-signature event still folds into state); `show`/`fsck` surface the display grouping (`verified`/`unverified`/`unsigned`) over the raw `verdict`.
- [ ] Default-install regression: with zero identity configuration, an integration test asserts a
      normal write succeeds unsigned, **no identity entity is minted**, and `list`/`ready`/gates behave
      identically to pre-feature (proves SC "default install unchanged").
- [ ] Snapshots (compaction) record the per-signed-event verification tuple `{event_uuid, content_hash,
      signature, signer_pubkey, position}` so compacted signed events still return `verified`
      without the original event object (round-trip test).
- [ ] Docs + ADR: identity model; the verdict enum (existing `verify_signature_result` verdicts + new `key_not_valid_at_era`) and its `verified/unverified/unsigned` display grouping; opt-in enforcement + the
      CI-merge-gate-is-the-control caveat + its detect-vs-reject limitation; Jira `accountId` mapping +
      the Modify-Reporter permission requirement; key-rotation / era (commit-ancestry: `added_at_commit`/`revoked_at_commit`);
      private-key storage (ssh-agent/`~/.ssh`, never committed); the provider-neutral seam.

## Intended decomposition (children)
1. `identity` ticket-type + entity (attributes, **public**-key-only storage + private-key guard,
   exemptions, normal + `create_placeholder` paths, config pointer, default-install invariant).
2. Event attribution (name+email + `author_id`) + replay back-compat + default-install regression test.
3. Authorship attestation kind on the foundation substrate (DSSE subject binding; unsigned fallback).
4. TOFU genesis + G2 signed key-rotation chain + era (commit-ancestry) validity.
5. Provider-neutral mapping + Jira adapter (outbound assignee/reporter, Modify-Reporter degrade).
6. Inbound ghost/placeholder + idempotent auto-upgrade.
7. Opt-in enforcement: local write-gate + **CI merge-gate verify job** + fsck `verified/unverified/unsigned`
   marking + replay-never-rejects + snapshot carries identities/sigs.
8. Docs + ADR.

## Prior art / grounding
git-bug identity entity + `ValidKeysAtTime` (log-position key validity) + bridge metadata; SCIM
`externalId`; OIDC `(iss,sub)` (email disqualified as key); Jira Cloud GDPR `accountId` (email
null/hidden) + Modify-Reporter permission; Gitea/GitLab placeholder-user + auto-upgrade; git `.mailmap`
(email as adopt-heuristic only). Depends on the asymmetric attestation foundation.
compiled_state.title: Authenticated identity — entity + authorship certification + provider-neutral user mapping
